items.q: What are the four Ps of the marketing mix?
items.correct: Product, Price, Place (distribution), and Promotion — the controllable variables a business uses to satisfy customers and achieve objectives.
items.misconception: The four Ps are Product, Price, Profit, and People.
items.random_wrong: The four Ps are Planning, Producing, Promoting, and Profit.
items.why: Students often substitute 'profit' or 'people' into the four Ps. The classic McCarthy framework is Product, Price, Place and Promotion — extended models add others but those four are foundational.

items.q: What is a brand in marketing?
items.correct: A combination of name, design, symbols and associations that distinguishes a product and creates customer perception and loyalty — more than just a logo.
items.misconception: A brand is just the logo on a product.
items.random_wrong: A brand is the company that makes a product.
items.why: Branding is the sum of perceptions, not just visual identity. Coca-Cola the brand is much more than the script logo — it is associations, taste expectations, advertising history.

items.correct: Holding minimal stock and receiving inputs only when needed for production — reducing storage costs but increasing vulnerability to supply disruption.
items.misconception: Just-in-time means delivering products to customers exactly on time.
items.random_wrong: Just-in-time means finishing work right before the deadline.
items.why: JIT is about INPUT stock to production, not output delivery to customers. Students misread the term as a delivery promise; it is an internal stock control philosophy.

items.q: What does the 'S' in SMART objectives stand for?
items.why: It's Specific. “Strategic” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which 1970 essay argued the 'social responsibility of business is to increase profits'?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Friedman. “Drucker” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many elements are in the SMART acronym?
items.why: The real figure is 5 elements. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
